notes_md:
**Từ từ** means *slowly* or *little by little*. It’s about reducing speed or doing something gently.

It’s an adverb, so it usually goes before or after a verb to describe how you do something.  

It can also stand alone as a soft command, like “wait” or “hold on.” In that sense, it’s similar to **khoan**.  
Opposite idea: **nhanh lên** (hurry up).
notes:
Từ từ means slowly or little by little. It’s about reducing speed or doing something gently.

It’s an adverb, so it usually goes before or after a verb to describe how you do something.  

It can also stand alone as a soft command, like “wait” or “hold on.” In that sense, it’s similar to khoan.  
Opposite idea: nhanh lên (hurry up).
exampleSentences: Em hãy từ từ ăn để không bị đau bụng nhé.
Chúng ta nên từ từ giải quyết vấn đề này để tránh sai lầm.
Anh lái xe từ từ khi trời mưa để đảm bảo an toàn.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Please eat slowly so you don't get a stomachache.
We should solve this problem slowly to avoid mistakes.
He drives slowly when it rains to ensure safety.

example.en: Please do it slowly so you don't make mistakes.
example.vi: Hãy làm từ từ để không bị sai.
example.es: Por favor, hazlo despacio para no cometer errores.
